title: raxis | 95 Dataplane bottlenecks (p50/p95/p99 across iter61 wire-sites)

panels.title: iter61 dataplane bottleneck wire-sites — p50 / p95 / p99 per pivot
Audit chain stage duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by stage
panels.description: Per-stage walk-cost in the on-disk audit chain. Source: `AuditChainStageDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-02`). Pivots on `stage` ∈ {`load_segment`, `verify_segment`, `walk_chain`}. A regression in `verify_segment` = signature batching slowed; in `walk_chain` = the chain grew faster than the bounded ring expected.

panels.title: Store SQL query duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by query_class (top-N)
panels.description: Per-query-class SQLite read latency on the dashboard read path. Source: `StoreQueryDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-01` + `-07`). The `query_class` lexicon is closed (`crates/store/src/observability.rs::QUERY_CLASSES`) so a sustained spike on one class localises a slow dashboard refresh to the underlying SQL — e.g. `audit_chain_walk` p99 climbing means the chain grew, `policy_snapshot` p99 climbing means the bundle projection grew with operator count.

panels.title: FSM event→commit duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by fsm_kind
panels.description: Wall-clock from FSM event-emit to audit-sink commit, pivoted on `fsm_kind` ∈ {`session`, `initiative`, `task`}. Source: `FsmTransitionDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-03`), wired in `kernel/src/notifications/sink.rs::bridge_audit_to_metric`. A regression localises a slow lifecycle to the FSM that owns the transition rather than the generic audit-append path. `from_state`/`to_state` labels are also present on the underlying samples — pivot on them in ad-hoc queries when one fsm_kind shows a tail spike.

panels.title: Git worktree stage duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by stage
panels.description: Host-side git provisioning sub-stage timings. Source: `GitWorktreeStageDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-04`), wired in `crates/worktree-provision/src/lib.rs`. Pivots on `stage` ∈ {`clone`, `fetch`, `checkout`, `verify`}. Both success AND error paths emit (the bottleneck signal is the error-path histogram, not just the happy path) — pivot on `outcome` in ad-hoc queries to separate slow-clones from fail-fast clones.

panels.title: Gateway stage duration (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99 by stage
panels.description: Per-stage gateway-internal timing. Source: `GatewayStageDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-06`). The transparent-proxy path emits `tproxy_admit`; the kernel-mediated planner fetch path emits `upstream_roundtrip` so provider fetches remain visible even when the guest has no NIC and no transparent-proxy traffic is exercised. Pivot on `outcome` in ad-hoc queries to split `ok`, provider errors, and policy-deny signals.

panels.title: IPC frame low-level codec latency (ms) — encode/decode by channel / direction
panels.description: Low-level CPU-side serialization/deserialization latency for bincode IPC frames. Source: `IpcFrameStageDuration` histogram (iter61 / `INV-OBSERVABILITY-DATAPLANE-LATENCY-05`), wired in `crates/ipc/src/frame.rs`, filtered to `stage=encode|decode`. Spikes here mean frame payloads or codec work grew. This is still useful low-level latency; it is just not end-to-end request latency. Use the `raxis | 15 IPC` substrate round-trip panels for kernel↔planner/verifier request latency by role/message_kind.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (stage, channel, direction, le) (sum_over_time(raxis_kernel_substrate_ipc_frame_stage_duration_milliseconds_bucket{stage=~"encode|decode"}[$__range])))
histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (stage, channel, direction, le) (sum_over_time(raxis_kernel_substrate_ipc_frame_stage_duration_milliseconds_bucket{stage=~"encode|decode"}[$__range])))
histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (stage, channel, direction, le) (sum_over_time(raxis_kernel_substrate_ipc_frame_stage_duration_milliseconds_bucket{stage=~"encode|decode"}[$__range])))

panels.title: IPC frame low-level socket latency/occupancy (ms) — read/write by channel / direction
panels.description: Low-level socket-stage latency/occupancy for bincode IPC frames. `read` is elapsed time waiting for the next complete frame from the peer; seconds here usually mean the peer has not produced/drained a frame, not that the kernel is slow at copying bytes. `write` captures socket drain/backpressure. Treat this as low-level idle/backpressure/stall occupancy. For true request latency, use the higher-level `KernelSubstrateIpcRoundtripDuration` panels in `raxis | 15 IPC`, pivoted by role/message_kind.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (stage, channel, direction, le) (sum_over_time(raxis_kernel_substrate_ipc_frame_stage_duration_milliseconds_bucket{stage=~"read|write"}[$__range])))
histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (stage, channel, direction, le) (sum_over_time(raxis_kernel_substrate_ipc_frame_stage_duration_milliseconds_bucket{stage=~"read|write"}[$__range])))
histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (stage, channel, direction, le) (sum_over_time(raxis_kernel_substrate_ipc_frame_stage_duration_milliseconds_bucket{stage=~"read|write"}[$__range])))
